109940.explanation: Gender equality in politics and society is important, and CDA Beesel, Reuver, Offenbeek encourages women's participation. However, we do not support an officially mandatory male-female distribution (a quota).

109941.explanation: CDA Beesel, Reuver, Offenbeek does not believe referendums are the solution for civic engagement. We advocate for other forms of participation in which residents can share their opinions, without the risks of polarization and delay associated with referendums.

109944.explanation: Farmers are food producers and stewards of nature and landscape. CDA Beesel sees room for initiatives such as regional sales or nature campsites, provided these are subordinate to the farming operation and fit within the environmental vision. In this way, farmers can contribute to the countryside and nature.

109945.explanation: There are already many subsidy streams for sustainable energy. Our position is that residents can draw from these streams, but the municipality of Beesel must actively promote awareness of them.

109947.explanation: CDA Beesel, Reuver, Offenbeek supports the implementation of the dispersion law, in which every municipality must contribute to shelter provision. In our view, this shelter should be small-scale and spread throughout the municipality, fitting the local living environment.

109950.explanation: People should be able to make their own choices in their own homes, as long as this does not lead to unacceptable nuisance. Wood smoke can be harmful; therefore, CDA Beesel, Reuver, Offenbeek advocates for conscious use and education instead of a ban.

109952.explanation: It costs a lot of money and is not a core task of the municipality. However, the municipality can support initiatives from residents or associations and ensure safety.

109955.explanation: Accessibility is important for everyone and strengthens inclusion. The CDA believes the municipality should support entrepreneurs through subsidies or advice to make buildings more accessible for people with disabilities. This is an investment in equal opportunities and a welcoming municipality.

109956.explanation: The zoning plan is leading and protects the landscape and spatial quality. Unrestricted building can lead to uncontrolled urbanization and loss of agricultural function. CDA chooses customization within existing rules, not a generic exemption.

109957.explanation: There are already schemes like the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture and Foundation Leergeld that support this. The CDA believes the municipality should actively promote these existing options and investigate whether further development, for example through the Adults Fund, is desirable.

109958.explanation: We consider the local broadcaster very important for communication to residents. In that light, we are pleased that a national standard will soon come into effect securing financial resources for the regional broadcaster.

109961.explanation: The CDA believes sports clubs are important for health and social cohesion, but financial support should be tailored. There are already schemes and subsidies available; the municipality can assist with advice and utilizing existing funds, not with structural extra contributions.

109964.explanation: Legally this is not feasible: CCTV must comply with strict legal requirements and is only allowed when there is a demonstrable concrete problem. Sometimes it may be desirable in cases of experienced insecurity, but then only tailored: temporary, targeted and with good justification and evaluation.

109967.explanation: We build according to need. For this target group, there is already sufficient supply according to the housing needs assessments. The CDA wants to avoid building without demand and opts for customization based on current figures.

109968.explanation: The CDA believes the zoning plan and environmental vision are leading. Permanent residence in recreational homes can lead to loss of tourism function and pressure on facilities.

109969.explanation: The CDA wants to keep village centers compact. We choose to build within or on the edge of the centers, and where possible restructuring or transformation. We are not in favor of isolated developments outside the centers.

109970.explanation: It would be good if people participate, but you cannot force it. The CDA chooses active dialogue and guidance to encourage people to participate, appropriate to their abilities, instead of a strict obligation.

109972.explanation: This is already arranged locally: Beesel applies 120% of the social assistance standard. This prevents people just above the threshold from getting into financial trouble. The CDA wants to maintain this approach to prevent poverty and debts.

109979.explanation: The dog tax contributes to financing facilities and balancing costs. The CDA believes this tax is justified as long as revenues are spent purposefully, for example on enforcement and public space.

109981.explanation: The CDA does not want random expansion of industrial sites, but also no complete stop. We choose to concentrate on suitable locations to preserve livability and village character, while giving entrepreneurs perspective.

109982.explanation: The CDA complies with legal rules, including the dispersal law and the obligation to house status holders. Priority is necessary to carry out this task and enable integration.

109983.explanation: There is already a separation between paid and unpaid factions. This may continue as is.

109986.explanation: We see school swimming as a valuable intervention, provided it always takes place in close cooperation with parents, education, swimming pools and clubs.

109994.explanation: The CDA does not want fewer parking spaces, but not arbitrarily more either. We choose to maintain sufficient parking and expand only if necessary. This keeps the center accessible and livable.

109996.explanation: The CDA wants to make a careful consideration based on the impact on Beesel. We are not inherently against new plans, but look at utility, necessity, environmental effects and employment. Decisions must fit within the environmental vision and the interests of residents.

109940.explanation: Samen Verder believes this should be the goal.

109941.explanation: Samen Verder believes this is fine for certain subjects.

109944.explanation: Samen Verder believes this is only possible if it fits within the environment.

109945.explanation: Samen Verder values sustainability highly and believes such initiatives should be supported.

109947.explanation: The municipality of Beesel already meets the number of asylum seekers to be accommodated.

109950.explanation: Samen Verder believes recreational use of a fireplace should remain possible.

109952.explanation: Samen Verder believes there should be a good alternative if fireworks for private individuals are banned. A light show, for example, could also work.

109956.explanation: Samen Verder believes this offers a good alternative for farmers who want to reduce their livestock and can help address the housing shortage.

109957.explanation: This is already happening in our municipality and Samen Verder believes it should continue that way.

109958.explanation: Samen Verder believes a local broadcaster plays a valuable role within our community.

109961.explanation: Samen Verder believes associations are very important for our community. We must support them.

109964.explanation: Samen Verder believes that in places known for undesirable activities, CCTV should be possible. For example, at the station.

109967.explanation: Samen Verder considers it important that elderly people can continue to live in our municipality. This is one of the priorities in our election program.

109968.explanation: Samen Verder believes this should be possible, but only for a limited period.

109969.explanation: Samen Verder values preserving a beautiful countryside and believes there is still sufficient possibility within the centers.

109970.explanation: Samen Verder believes this can encourage people to keep participating in society and help them (re)gain steady employment.

109972.explanation: Samen Verder considers poverty a major problem. We must help these people.

109979.explanation: Samen Verder believes this tax is not necessary.

109981.explanation: Samen Verder does not want new heavy industry. We want to locate industrial sites as much as possible outside the centers.

109982.explanation: Samen Verder believes that refugees living within the municipality should, like other residents, be given priority over people from outside the municipality.

109983.explanation: Samen Verder believes that waste separation should be encouraged. This service is very important to many residents.

109986.explanation: Samen Verder finds it important that all children can maintain their swimming skills, and moreover, school swimming is also very enjoyable.

109994.explanation: Samen Verder considers the number of parking spaces to be appropriate.

109996.explanation: Samen Verder finds this important because our municipality suffers from dirty air emissions. We participate in the Clean Air Agreement.

109940.explanation: This is independent of whether someone is a woman or a man; they must possess the right qualities.

109941.explanation: In specific issues, a referendum can be a solution. But for very complex matters, that is not always appropriate.

109944.explanation: The environmental plan outlines the prospects for nature and recreation in the rural area.

109945.explanation: We continue to support residents in making their homes more sustainable. We have a do-it-yourself subsidy for residents who want to insulate their homes independently.

109947.explanation: At the moment, we already do a lot regarding shelter in our municipality; we will have to look together with our closest larger municipalities to determine who implements which form of shelter. This must be balanced with the scale and possibilities we can meet.

109950.explanation: We will continue to allow this in our municipality, but first clear regulations must be established on this theme. Once they are in place, we will enforce them, not before.

109952.explanation: We will have to enforce regulations on fireworks.

109955.explanation: We have a policy that oversees the accessibility of buildings for people with disabilities.

109956.explanation: We are in favor of active land policy. However, we want to maintain control over how we implement this.

109957.explanation: We promote our ambition to become the healthiest region and for that we encourage exercise and thus provide the opportunity to participate in sports.

109958.explanation: We see school swimming as a valuable measure, provided this takes place in close collaboration with parents, education, swimming pools, and clubs. Preferably it becomes part of the extended school day, so children can exercise and learn to swim in an accessible way, with extra attention for children who do not yet have a diploma.

109961.explanation: We have short lines of communication with the clubs, which also makes it possible to intervene in time before financial problems arise. We believe our clubs are an important part of the community. They contribute to livability and must be supported with subsidies and financial aid where necessary.

109964.explanation: We want to counter this by focusing more on a safe living environment.

109967.explanation: To promote the flow of elderly people who currently occupy large homes, it is important that they can move into smaller rental or owner-occupied homes. This means we must ensure the right types of housing so these homes become available for young families.

109968.explanation: We do not consider this a desirable situation.

109969.explanation: At this moment, we still have enough locations within the municipal boundaries to meet the housing demand.

109970.explanation: For us, it is important that these people reintegrate into society. We provide intensive guidance and look at what someone can still do. We motivate them to take steps repeatedly to return to society.

109979.explanation: To maintain a clean and beautiful living environment, we must offer and maintain facilities to clean up dog waste. This costs money.

109981.explanation: Yes, but no longer on industrial estates within built-up areas (city center).

109982.explanation: With the new housing allocation regulation, a fixed percentage (50% of allocations by housing corporations) already provides for allocation based on local ties and involvement; this also applies to refugees.

109983.explanation: By focusing on as much waste separation and reuse of raw materials as possible, the amount of residual waste will decrease and the costs for this will be small.

109986.explanation: We see school swimming as a valuable measure, provided this happens in close cooperation with parents and education. This is something that should be addressed nationally.

109994.explanation: We invest heavily in a beautiful appearance of our center, with more terraces and hospitality venues. No parking spaces belong in this center. We offer free parking within walking distance in the outskirts. We encourage cycling by providing good and sufficient parking facilities.

109996.explanation: We are neither initiator nor authorized, but we have expressed our concerns about the effects this plan may have on our living environment. We are actively kept informed about the plans and their potential impact on air quality. Opposition is only possible if permitted regulations are not met and monitoring fails.

statements.title: The municipality of Beesel should try to stop new industrial plans at Zevenellen.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: More small rental and owner-occupied homes should be built in Beesel for the elderly.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Beesel should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The residents of Beesel should be able to give their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Beesel moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: The business parks in Beesel may be allowed to expand.
Dropping off waste at the recycling centre should remain free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves in Beesel should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Beesel moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: There should be fewer parking spaces in the center of Beesel.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.
